opts: red liquid flowing in the body
quiz.explanation: Blood is the red liquid that flows through the body carrying oxygen and nutrients.
Burn means to be on fire or to be destroyed by fire.

opts: smallest unit of a living organism
quiz.explanation: Cell is the smallest structural and functional unit of a living organism.
Contain means to have something inside or to hold something within.
Correct means free from error; true or accurate.
Crop means a plant or agricultural produce grown and harvested by farmers.
Demand means a strong request or a need for something.

opts: the same in amount or value
quiz.explanation: Equal means the same in size, value, amount, or status.
Feed means to give food to a person, animal, or plant.
Hole means an opening, gap, or hollow space in something.
Increase means to become or make greater in size, amount, or number.
Lord means a person who has power, authority, or ownership over others.

opts: to be required to pay or give
quiz.explanation: Owe means to be under obligation to pay or give something to someone.
Position means the place where someone or something is located, or a rank in a job.

opts: to lift up or bring up
quiz.explanation: Raise means to lift something upward or to bring up a child with care.

opts: having a duty to deal with something
quiz.explanation: Responsible means being the person who caused something or having a duty to manage it.

opts: ability to see or something seen
quiz.explanation: Sight means the ability to see, or something that is seen.
Spot means a particular place or a small mark on a surface.

opts: the way something is built or organized
quiz.explanation: Structure means the way in which something is built, arranged, or organized.
Whole means complete, entire, or all of something without any part missing.

story.title: The Responsible Farmer
story.body: In a small village surrounded by green fields, there lived a farmer named Alam. He was known as the most responsible person in the whole village. Every season, he grew crops on a large piece of land that his lord had given him to manage. Alam knew that he owed everything — his position, his home, and his family's food — to the hard work he put into the soil every single day. One morning, Alam noticed a hole in the wall of the storage room where he kept his harvest. Rats had entered and were beginning to destroy the crop. Without wasting time, he fixed the hole, saved the remaining grain, and made a correct decision to build a stronger structure for storage. His blood, sweat, and effort went into every corner of his farm. He would raise his children near the fields so they could learn the value of the land from an early sight. He always told them, 'The whole earth feeds us — we must take care of it.' The demand for Alam's crops increased every year because of their quality. He made sure to feed every poor family in the village during difficult times, believing that all people are equal and deserve to eat. He burned the dry waste from the fields to make space for new growth, never wasting a single spot of land. Scientists once visited the village and were amazed by the cell-like structure of Alam's farming system — each section connected and working together perfectly. They said his method contained the best practices of sustainable farming. As the years passed, Alam's position in the community grew. He became a symbol of hard work, equality, and responsibility. His children raised their own families with the same values, and the sight of their green fields became the most beautiful spot in the entire region.

comprehension.q: What did Alam find in the storage room?
What did Alam do to save his crop?
opts: he fixed the hole and built a stronger structure

comprehension.q: Why did the demand for his crops increase?
What did Alam believe about all people?
opts: all people are equal and deserve to eat

comprehension.q: What did scientists say about his farming?
opts: it contained the best practices of sustainable farming
it needed to increase in size

listening.title: A Doctor Explains the Human Body
listening.script:
A: Doctor, can you explain the structure of the human body simply?
B: Of course. The whole body is made of tiny units called cells.
A: What do cells do?
B: Each cell has a correct position and contains specific material to do its job.
A: What about blood?
B: Blood is very important. It feeds every cell by carrying oxygen and nutrients.
A: Can the demand for blood increase?
B: Yes, during illness or injury, the body needs more blood cells.
A: What happens when a cell burns or is damaged?
B: The body raises its defense system to repair the spot.
A: Is the body responsible for healing itself?
B: Absolutely. A healthy body is an equal and balanced structure.
A: Can sight be affected by blood?
B: Yes. If blood flow to the eyes is reduced, sight can be lost.
A: So we owe our health to our cells?
B: Exactly. We must raise awareness about healthy habits.
A: What should we do to increase health?
B: Eat whole foods, exercise, and be responsible for your body every day.
A: Thank you, doctor.
B: Remember, the whole body works as one. Take care of every part.

comprehension.q: What is the whole body made of?

opts: it feeds every cell with oxygen

comprehension.q: What happens when a cell is damaged?
opts: the body raises its defense to repair the spot

comprehension.q: What does the doctor advise to increase health?
opts: eat whole foods and be responsible

speaking.prompt: Talk about what it means to be responsible. Use words like responsible, position, raise, equal, demand, correct, and whole.
speaking.example: Being responsible means doing the correct thing even when it is difficult. In my position as a student, I must raise my knowledge every day. I believe all people are equal and we owe it to each other to be kind. The whole community benefits when everyone is responsible. The demand for good people is always high, so I try to improve myself every day.
tips: Define what responsibility means to you
Give real examples from your life
Use words like owe, raise, position, and equal
Talk about how responsibility affects the whole community
Mention what happens when people are not responsible
Speak clearly and use full sentences
